title: Qi Men Dun Jia — The Highest Level of Chinese Metaphysics
summary: Qi Men Dun Jia is considered the "Emperor's Art" and the highest form of Chinese divination.
content:
Qi Men Dun Jia (奇门遁甲) is the most advanced form of Chinese divination, traditionally used for military strategy and high-stakes decision-making.

【The Name】
Qi = the three special stems (Bing, Ding, Yi). Men = the eight gates. Dun = to conceal. Jia = the leading Heavenly Stem. Together: "using the special energies and gates to conceal and protect."

【The Eight Gates】
- Rest Gate (休): Good for rest, wealth, marriage
- Birth Gate (生): Excellent for starting ventures, business
- Hurt Gate (伤): Bad for business, good for hunting
- Barrier Gate (杜): Neutral, good for hiding, military
- Scene Gate (景): Good for exams, writing, tourism
- Death Gate (死): Unfavorable for most activities
- Alarm Gate (惊): Good for legal matters, bad for travel
- Open Gate (开): Excellent for openings, travel, fame

【Applications】
Originally created for warfare, Qi Men is now used for: choosing favorable dates and directions, business strategy and investment timing, and personal decision-making.

title: Introduction to Qimen Dunjia Chart Setup
summary: Understand the basic principles of Qimen Dunjia chart setup, and learn the distribution patterns of the Nine Palaces, Eight Gates, Nine Stars, and Eight Spirits.
content:
The chart setup of Qimen Dunjia may seem complex, but it follows a set of strict logical rules.

【Nine Palaces Grid】
The foundation of Qimen chart setup is a Nine Palaces grid, corresponding to the numbers of the Luoshu Nine Palaces:
Xun 4 Palace   Li 9 Palace   Kun 2 Palace
Zhen 3 Palace   Center 5 Palace   Dui 7 Palace
Gen 8 Palace   Kan 1 Palace   Qian 6 Palace

【Time Parameters】
Chart setup first requires determining the time parameters, i.e., the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches of the year, month, day, and hour. The most commonly used is the Hour-based Qimen.

【Four Layers】
A complete Qimen chart consists of four layers:
1. Earth Plate (Nine Palaces) — Fixed and unchanging
2. Heaven Plate (Nine Stars) — Rotates with the Heavenly Stems
3. Human Plate (Eight Gates) — Rotates with the hour
4. Spirit Plate (Eight Spirits) — Rotates with the solar terms

【Yin and Yang Dun】
Yang Dun: From the Winter Solstice to the Summer Solstice, Yang energy rises, and the sequence of bureaus is arranged in forward order.
Yin Dun: From the Summer Solstice to the Winter Solstice, Yin energy rises, and the sequence of bureaus is arranged in reverse order.

【Beginner Advice】
Learning Qimen Dunjia can be divided into three steps: Memorize the attributes of the Nine Palaces and Eight Gates → Master the Yin and Yang Dun and the bureaus of solar terms → Learn to analyze the relationships of generation and control in the chart.
